pressure_source: Magic is dying and every conventional avenue is exhausted; the only remaining lead requires entering the site of her worst trauma
state_shift: Hermione moves from contained researcher to someone who must enter the Manor — the decision costs her control and sleep
must_land_beats: Establish dying magic through specific daily failures, not exposition
Show Hermione's competence-as-armor: divorced, alone, work as identity
The Mudblood scar glimpsed once — sleeve adjustment, glamour — establishing concealment as habit
The Malfoy library proposition arrives and she decides to go
secondary_character_beats: Harry present through brief contact — a fire-call or note — warm but she deflects his concern

setups_to_plant.description: Hermione adjusts her sleeve or checks a glamour — habitual, automatic, establishing the scar as something she hides from everyone
Specific spell failures and institutional strain that establish the scale of the crisis

pressure_source: Two people with brutal shared history must negotiate terms of sustained proximity; the Manor is already responding to her presence
state_shift: Terms set — they are bound to proximity. The house has already started changing, and Draco notices but doesn't mention it
must_land_beats: Draco's isolation rendered concretely — the Manor too large, his life too small, careful and precise and starved
Hermione arrives; the negotiation is exact, professional, last-name-basis
The Dark Mark's new behavior — pain without signal, magic restless
The Manor opens a forgotten corridor when she enters — Draco registers this silently
secondary_character_beats: Narcissa referenced in passing — her absence from the Manor noted as recent, deliberate

setups_to_plant.description: The house opens a corridor Draco forgot existed the moment Hermione enters — first sign of differential treatment
The drawing room is not mentioned but Draco's awareness of specific rooms — which ones he avoids, which corridors he doesn't take — establishes that the Manor's geography is emotionally loaded

pressure_source: The first physical contact through Mark examination, and the house revealing itself as a sentient actor
state_shift: Physical contact established between them. The Manor is alive and aware, and Hermione is inside something that watches her
must_land_beats: First Mark examination — clinical frame, her hands on his forearm, his rigidity, the Mark responding to her diagnostic spells
The library: extraordinary and chaotic, centuries of uncatalogued acquisition
Alone in the Manor, the house shifts — she gets genuinely lost for twenty minutes before the corridors release her

setups_to_plant.description: The Mark responds to Hermione's diagnostic spells specifically — the dark magic in it behaves differently under her touch than under any previous examination

pressure_source: The Manor is treating Hermione differently than it has ever treated Draco — doors open for her, shelves rearrange for her, the house's attention shifts to her
state_shift: Draco recognizes the Manor is responding to Hermione specifically. Narcissa visits and her reaction confirms this is significant and not new to her
must_land_beats: Draco observes the Manor responding to Hermione — concrete details: doors, shelves, warmth in walls
Narcissa visits; she notices the shifts with recognition, not surprise, and gives minimal explanation
secondary_character_beats: Narcissa's ease with the Manor — touching walls, pausing at doorways — suggests a long and complicated relationship with the house that predates Hermione's arrival

setups_to_plant.description: Narcissa recognizes the Manor's behavior but deflects with a careful non-answer. Her expression when she sees an anomalous door is recognition, not surprise

pressure_source: The Mark resonance begins — she can feel him at a distance — and she conceals this from both of them while their intellectual intimacy deepens
state_shift: The Mark bond is active. She can sense his proximity and pain. She attributes it to diagnostic residue and hides it
must_land_beats: Library research scenes — intellectual sparring, mutual respect, the pleasure of rigorous collaboration
First Mark resonance at distance: she knows which floor he is on, feels a low ache that is not hers
Scene with Harry outside the Manor — warm, real friendship; she lies about how she is sleeping
Scar concealment: sleeve adjusted twice during Harry conversation
secondary_character_beats: Harry's protectiveness is real but checked by his knowledge that Hermione resents being managed. He asks about sleep, not about Draco

setups_to_plant.description: Hermione feels the Mark's pain and Draco's proximity at a distance. She rationalizes it as diagnostic artifact and tells no one

pressure_source: The Manor gates access to critical research behind a proximity requirement — the archive room holds only when both are present
state_shift: The Manor's rules are explicit. Access to what they need requires togetherness. The house is treating their connection as currency
must_land_beats: Mark examination with deeper probing — the clinical frame harder to hold, his pulse under her fingers, the dark magic quieting specifically under her touch
Discovery of the gated archive: the room closes when one leaves, holds when both are present. They test this empirically and the result is unambiguous

payoffs_to_land.description: The differential treatment escalates from opening corridors to gating entire rooms behind proximity
The Mark's specific responsiveness to Hermione deepens — it quiets under sustained contact in a way that suggests the magic recognizes her

pressure_source: Narcissa carries knowledge about the Manor's bonding history that she reveals obliquely — enough to unsettle, not enough to resolve
state_shift: The Manor has agenda and precedent. Narcissa's lived experience adds a generational layer to the house's behavior. The magical decay research points to deep structural damage
must_land_beats: Narcissa and Hermione alone — careful, layered, two intelligent women circling. Narcissa's history with the house rendered as exhausted intimacy, not mystique
Narcissa's warning: the house is not malicious but not neutral
Research progress: magical decay tied to war damage to ancient anchoring infrastructure; the Malfoys helped build it
secondary_character_beats: Narcissa as a full person: her social position (neither pardoned nor convicted), her awareness that the house treated her marriage differently, her protective instinct toward Draco that she expresses through strategic partial truth

payoffs_to_land.description: Narcissa gives partial information about the Manor's bonding history — enough to seed doubt without naming the mechanism

setups_to_plant.description: What Narcissa holds back here — the Manor cannot fabricate connection, only amplify it — is the specific knowledge that will reframe the rupture in Ch18

pressure_source: The Mark floods shared memory during an examination — Draco relives taking the Mark at sixteen, and Hermione feels it through the resonance
state_shift: She has seen his worst moment through his own sensory experience. The clinical frame between examiner and subject is permanently broken
must_land_beats: The Mark memory flood: specific, sensory — Voldemort's study, sixteen years old, his father's hand, the pain that made him pass out. Hermione feels the fear and the age of the boy
The aftermath silence — not careful distance but the quiet of two people who have been inside the same pain

payoffs_to_land.description: The resonance that began as faint proximity-awareness in Ch5 now transmits full sensory memory — the bond's capacity has deepened

pressure_source: The drawing room appears — the room where Hermione was tortured and Draco watched — at the moment she is closest to feeling safe in the Manor
state_shift: The war's worst moment between them is on the table. The drawing room cannot be pretended away. Civil distance is no longer available
must_land_beats: Hermione's guard lowering — small moment of unguarded interiority before the room appears
The drawing room: specific, sensory, the trauma preserved in merciless detail — chandelier, marble, cold
The reckoning: not clean. She tells him what she remembers. He tells her what he remembers. Neither handles it well. Unfair things said. Inadequate things said. They leave separately

payoffs_to_land.description: The room Draco has been avoiding manifests for Hermione at maximum vulnerability. The emotional geography of the Manor made concrete

pressure_source: The aftermath of trauma confrontation — both characters navigating a new register of honesty while trying to continue the work
state_shift: Tenderness enters without permission. Domesticity becomes habitual. A third register emerges between them: not professional, not traumatic, but quiet and almost fond
must_land_beats: Post-drawing-room gentleness rendered in small domestic moments: shared meals, books left on chairs, tea made without asking
Draco cataloguing Hermione's expressions — her laugh, her sleeping in the library — registering that he is doing this
Theo Nott's visit: Draco with a friend, drier and funnier, the dynamic revealing who he is when not armored
secondary_character_beats: Theo Nott as a full person: post-war Slytherin navigating suspicion, pragmatic, bitter in flashes, genuinely loyal. He sees the situation with Hermione immediately and says almost nothing about it except one honest line to Draco alone

pressure_source: The bonding texts reveal the Manor as a bonding artifact — every moment of genuine connection is now suspect. The central doubt crystallizes
state_shift: The central question is articulated and named. Hermione retreats. The information asymmetry that will fuel the rupture begins — she does not tell Draco what she found
must_land_beats: Mark resonance now invasive — mutual peripheral sensation during examination, her flinch at the vulnerability
Discovery of bonding texts: the Manor designed to forge connections, amplify resonance, gate access based on bond strength
Her retreat: she tells herself she needs to verify before alarming him. She knows this is a lie

setups_to_plant.description: Hermione finds the bonding texts and does not tell Draco. Her rationalization and the fact that she continues proximity while concealing this knowledge must be clearly established as the specific betrayal he will later identify

pressure_source: Hermione's withdrawal is incomprehensible to Draco; the scar reveal during a Mark examination strips the last layer of concealment between them
state_shift: He has seen the full cost of what happened in his house. The power dynamic shifts — she is no longer the competent researcher helping the damaged heir. They are both marked
must_land_beats: Draco's confusion and hurt at her withdrawal — he interprets it as rejection and retreats into his own distance
The Manor responds to their mutual withdrawal: corridors lengthen, archive won't open, the house goes cold
The scar reveal: glamour flickers or sleeve rides up during examination. MUDBLOOD carved in her forearm. He touches it. She flinches. His hand stays
The scene that follows: the most intimate so far. No kiss. He traces the letters. The silence. His honesty and her question

payoffs_to_land.description: The scar she has been hiding since Ch1 is seen by the person whose house it was carved in. The concealment habit established across multiple chapters makes the exposure land with full weight

pressure_source: The outside world intrudes — Ron's worry, Harry's observation, the magical decay going public, rumors about her presence at the Manor
state_shift: The world outside the Manor still exists and is watching. The decay's escalation raises research stakes. Her situation has a public dimension she has been ignoring
must_land_beats: Ron scene: specific, not plot-functional. Their divorce rendered as the real, undramatic thing it was. His worry specific to her patterns, not to jealousy
Magical decay worsening: Floo failures, healing rationed at St. Mungo's, accidental magic spikes in children
Harry tells her people are noticing. Rumors. Her movements are observed
She returns to the Manor and the house opens for her with warmth — the ease of that welcome disturbs her
secondary_character_beats: Ron as a full person: building a life without Hermione, mostly succeeding. His concern is the specific pain of watching someone you loved return to the place that hurt them
Harry relays Ginny's perspective — sharper, more pragmatic, not diplomatic

payoffs_to_land.description: The decay established as background in Ch1 is now public crisis — infrastructure failures, institutional strain, children's magic destabilizing

pressure_source: The scar revelation cracked something open; Hermione has returned to proximity. The deep archive opens further and the collaborative work is the best version of what they are together
state_shift: Draco stops resisting what he feels. The house is at its most seductive. The intellectual and emotional connection are no longer separable
must_land_beats: Her return to proximity after Ch11-12's withdrawal — not full trust, but present
Deeper archive: texts on original magical infrastructure construction, decodable only by combining his family knowledge with her theory
The Manor at its most seductive — warm, golden, stable, generous — creating conditions where togetherness feels natural
Draco watches her work and stops pretending he is not watching

pressure_source: Weeks of proximity, shared trauma, intellectual partnership, and mutual want collapse into physical contact — but the doubt about the Manor's influence makes the moment simultaneously desired and suspect
state_shift: Physical threshold crossed. The doubt intensifies rather than resolving — desire and suspicion now coexist in every interaction
must_land_beats: The first kiss: not during a dramatic moment. In the library, late, tired, close over a text. Clumsy at first because they are both terrified
The kiss rendered specifically — hands, breath, the quality of the want. Not a fade-to-black
The doubt in her body during and after: is this the Manor? Is this amplification? The resonance singing between them
Afterward alone: the Manor is the warmest it has ever been and she cannot trust the warmth

pressure_source: Draco finds the bonding texts independently and realizes Hermione has known for weeks — the information asymmetry becomes the specific blade of betrayal
state_shift: The relationship breaks. Both retreat to oldest defenses. The betrayal is not the bond — it is the secret she kept about the bond
must_land_beats: Draco in the archive alone — the room holds briefly, unstable. He finds the bonding texts
The realization: she has known. Her withdrawal, her careful return, the kiss — all under knowledge she hoarded
The confrontation: his anger specific to the information asymmetry, not the bond itself. She stayed. She kissed him. She treated their connection as something to study and verify
Her defense real but insufficient. His deeper wound: even this was done to him. Her deeper wound: being accused of calculation when she was drowning
She leaves the Manor

payoffs_to_land.description: The secret Hermione kept since Ch11 is discovered. The specific betrayal: she continued proximity and initiated intimacy while concealing knowledge that put their entire connection in doubt

pressure_source: Separation. The Mark is worse without her sustained contact. The magical decay accelerates. The research is unfinished. The cost of apart is concrete
state_shift: The cost of separation is measurable — personal, magical, structural. Hermione begins to understand what she did and why. Harry says the hard true thing
must_land_beats: Hermione in her flat: the ordinary silence, the absence of the Manor's responsiveness, the gutted feeling
Mark resonance persists at distance — she can feel his pain, the Mark thrashing without her sustained contact
Magical decay accelerating: Floo down for hours, accidental magic spikes, institutional crisis
Harry's honest line: the thing she is angry about is not what she thinks it is
She sits with the truth of what she concealed and why
secondary_character_beats: Harry as the person who has earned the right to say hard things. His line should cut through her self-narrative without being cruel

pressure_source: Narcissa's revelation reframes the bond question; the choice to return is made despite unresolved doubt because separation has its own distortions
state_shift: They choose the uncertainty together. The drawing room entered by choice. The first-name shift lands. Book 1 closes with forward momentum — the architecture is involuntary but the choice to inhabit it is not
must_land_beats: Draco alone in the cold Manor — the house inert, the Mark at its worst, the corridors sealed

Narcissa's revelation: the Manor cannot fabricate connection, only amplify what exists. It cannot manufacture love any more than a greenhouse can manufacture seeds. This reframes without fully resolving
Narcissa's additional disclosure: Lucius never resonated with the house the way Draco does with Hermione
The return: a choice, not a resolution. The doubt persists but they choose to face it together
The drawing room: they enter it together
The first-name transition: quiet, private, irreversible

payoffs_to_land.description: What Narcissa withheld across the novel — that the bond requires genuine feeling, that amplification is not fabrication — reframes every moment of doubt
The drawing room that appeared at her most vulnerable in Ch9 is now entered by shared choice — the emotional geography of the Manor reclaimed
